batch_summary: Chandler wakes up in terror to find his eccentric roommate, Eddie, watching him sleep. When Eddie unsettlingly reveals he has secretly watched and hidden from Chandler on previous nights, a disturbed Chandler demands that Eddie move out immediately. Eddie reluctantly agrees to leave by the next day. After Eddie exits, Chandler blows a raspberry, only for Eddie to call him out from the hallway. Later, the scene transitions to a coffee shop where Rachel, Monica, and Joey gather.
cumulative_summary: Chandler's night takes a creepy turn when he wakes up to find his roommate, Eddie, sitting by his bed and watching him sleep. When Chandler demands to know why, Eddie unsettlingly explains that it makes him feel peaceful. The situation escalates when Eddie admits to hiding behind the door on previous nights to watch Chandler undetected. Terrified and fed up, Chandler demands that Eddie move out of the apartment immediately. Eddie defensively agrees to pack up and leave for his brother's basement by the time Chandler returns from work the next day. As Eddie exits, Chandler blows a childish raspberry, but is instantly unnerved when Eddie calls out that he heard it. The scene then transitions to a local coffee shop during the day, where Rachel, wearing her apron, approaches Monica on the sofa just as Joey walks in, shifting the focus to the broader group of friends.

batch_summary: At the coffee shop, Joey remains surprisingly upbeat after losing his job, showing off a new hat and using sophisticated vocabulary he learned from Chandler's word-of-the-day toilet paper. After Joey steps away, Phoebe joins Monica and Rachel, enthusiastically introducing them to a feminist self-help book called Be Your Own Windkeeper. Phoebe explains the book's concepts of female empowerment, describing women as wind-goddesses whose power is stolen by men, leaving Rachel intrigued by the philosophy.
